title: Rotini CLI Spec Schema
description: Combined schema for a Rotini CLI spec file (.rotini.json / .rotini.yaml)

$schema.description: URL identifying the rotini schema version. The version segment must match the installed rotini binary version.

commands.description: The root command definition. Has the same structure as any command node.

configuration_files.description: Config files to load at startup.

env_prefix.description: Prefix prepended to all auto-derived environment variable names (e.g. 'MYAPP' → 'MYAPP_FLAG_NAME'). Must be uppercase A-Z/0-9/_ with no trailing underscore.

metadata.description: Build-time metadata vars injected via go ldflags. Rotini generates typed var declarations in the framework package and a Metadata struct field on CLIMetadata, accessible in all command handlers via spec.Metadata.

schemas.description: Reusable named schema definitions. Referenced elsewhere via "$ref": "#/schemas/<Name>".

var.description: Exported Go identifier for the ldflag var. Rotini declares this as a package-level var in the framework package. Inject at build time: -ldflags "-X <module>/<framework-pkg>.<var>=<value>"

default.description: Fallback value used when the ldflag has not been applied at build time. Becomes the var's initial value in the generated file.

BaseSchema.description: Shared fields for Schema and InputSchema. JSON Schema Draft 7 does not support additionalProperties: false on schemas that use allOf for inheritance — strictness is enforced by Go's DisallowUnknownFields at parse time.

$ref.description: Reference to a named schema in the top-level schemas map (e.g. '#/schemas/MySchema'). Resolved at codegen time.

type.description: The type used to parse and store the value. Accepts both Go type names (bool, int, float64, []string, duration, map) and JSON Schema standard names (boolean, integer, number, array, object) — both are equivalent.

enum.description: Allowed values; parsed values are validated against this list

pattern.description: Regular expression the value must match (string type only)

Schema.description: JSON Schema-inspired type definition used for output/response and object property schemas. The 'required' field is a string array of required property names (JSON Schema object semantics). For input schemas where 'required' means 'must be provided', use InputSchema instead.

required.description: Required property names for object schemas (standard JSON Schema semantics).

InputSchema.description: Extended schema for input definitions (flags, arguments, env vars, config values, stdin). Inherits all BaseSchema fields and adds input-level metadata. The 'required' field here is a boolean indicating whether this input must be provided — unlike Schema where 'required' is a string array of property names.

required.description: When true, the input must be provided (or stdin must not be empty for stdin inputs). Note: this is a boolean — unlike the string-array 'required' on Schema.

default.description: Default value applied when the input is not provided

file.description: Config file logical name to read from (config_values inputs only)

key.description: Key path within the config file (config_values inputs only, e.g., 'server.port')

FlagConstraints.description: Declares mutual-exclusion or co-requirement grouping for a flag. All flags that share a group name and constraint type are evaluated together at parse time. A flag may belong to at most one constraint group.

xor.description: Group name. At most one flag in this group may be provided.

and.description: Group name. All flags in this group must be provided together, or none.

one_of.description: Group name. Exactly one flag in this group must be provided.

$ref.description: Reference to a named schema in the top-level schemas map (e.g. '#/schemas/MySchema'). When set, the schema is inlined at codegen time and $ref should not also be set.

schema.description: Type definition and input-level metadata (required, deprecated, default, hidden)

identifiers.description: CLI flag identifiers (e.g., '--force', '-f'). When absent, '--<name>' is auto-derived.

constraints.description: Mutual-exclusion or co-requirement group membership for this flag.

schema.description: Type definition and input-level metadata (required, deprecated, default)

name.description: Logical name for this config value

schema.description: Type definition and input-level metadata (required, default, file, key)

name.description: Logical name for this env var input

schema.description: Type definition and input-level metadata (required, default, variable)
Type definition for stdin content. Set required: true in schema to error when stdin is empty.

arguments.description: Positional argument inputs for this command

config_values.description: Config file value inputs for this command

environment.description: Environment variable inputs for this command

stdin.description: Declares expected stdin format and schema for this command

key.description: Unique event identifier (snake_case). Used to name Emit{Key} methods and On{Key} handler fields.

msg.description: Shape of the event payload. Uses the same schema as command output: fields array for a struct, or a scalar type. When omitted, the emit method takes a *{BinName}{PascalKey}EventMsg of an empty struct.

behavior.description: fail_fast stops at the first validation error (default); fail_collect gathers all errors before returning

name.description: Name of the remote command. The dispatched binary must be named <program>-<name> and located in the same directory as the host binary.

aliases.description: Additional names that invoke this remote command.

timeout.description: Host-side timeout for the remote binary execution. Uses Go duration format (e.g. "10s", "1m30s"). Empty or omitted means no timeout.

name.description: Logical name for the config file (e.g., 'app-config')

schema.description: When set, generates a typed config struct and validates required fields at load time.

hot_reload.description: When true, watches the config file for changes and reloads at runtime without restarting the process.

hot_reload_debounce.description: Debounce window for hot-reload file-change events. Uses Go duration format (e.g. '200ms'). Defaults to '200ms' when omitted.

Command.description: A command node in the CLI command tree.

aliases.description: Additional names that invoke this command. Command aliases affect dispatch routing; use identifiers on flags for flag aliases.

timeout.description: Command execution timeout. Uses Go duration format (e.g. "10s", "1m30s"). Empty or omitted means no timeout.

input.description: Typed inputs for this command: flags, arguments, configuration file values, and environment variables.

remote_commands.description: Co-located remote binaries dispatched as first-class sub-commands of this command.

events.description: Events this command can emit. Each key must be unique across the entire spec.

package.description: Import path (relative to module root) for the command handler package. Package name is derived from the last path segment.

gen_file.description: Name of the framework-controlled gen file placed in the command handler package (e.g. 'cmdr.gen.go'). Must end in .go.

PruneConfig.description: Controls pruning of stub files in the command handler package.

enabled.description: When true, rotini removes stub files in the command handler package that no longer correspond to a command in the spec.

keep.description: File names (not paths) that should never be removed by pruning, even when they do not correspond to a command in the spec.

package.description: Import path (relative to module root) for the framework package. Package name is derived from the last path segment.

gen_file.description: Name of the framework gen file placed in the framework package (e.g. 'rotini.gen.go'). Must end in .go.

additional_imports.description: Additional Go import paths included in the generated framework file. Use when input or output types reference third-party packages. Each entry is a package path (e.g., 'net/url') or an aliased import (e.g., 'myalias net/url').
